entities.name: SORGHUM S 35
entities.description: A nonphotoperiod-sensitive, high-yielding, early-maturing, and drought-tolerant pure line of sorghum originating from ICRISAT)

entities.name: NONPHOTOPERIOD-SENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to flower regardless of day length)

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The capacity to produce a large amount of crop per unit area)

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The characteristic of reaching maturity quickly)

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand water-deficient conditions)

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the S 35 variety originated from ICRISAT's breeding program)

entities.name: CAMEROON
entities.description: A country where the S 35 variety was advanced and promoted)

entities.name: CHAD
entities.description: A country where the S 35 variety was introduced and its economic impact assessed)

entities.name: DROUGHT-PRONE AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by frequent water scarcity)

entities.name: NET PRESENT VALUE
entities.description: A financial metric used to estimate the value of research investments)

entities.name: INTERNAL RATE OF RETURN
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the profitability of an investment)

entities.name: GERMPLASM RESEARCH SPILLOVERS
entities.description: The transfer of knowledge and genetic resources between breeding programs)

entities.name: MINI-DOSES APPROACH
entities.description: A seed distribution strategy used to reach farmers)

entities.name: GASSI
entities.description: The site of the FAO/UNDP-supported seed project)

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Simple, low-capital techniques recommended for the adoption of S 35)

entities.name: YIELD ADVANTAGE
entities.description: The increase in production compared to local varieties)

entities.name: BIRD ATTACK
entities.description: A constraint where birds damage the crop)

entities.name: LOW SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: A soil-related constraint affecting crop growth)
